title: Self-Attention in Action
concept: Self-attention builds rich representations where every word's vector is updated based on its neighbors in the sentence. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Seq2Seq Models.'

exercises.prompt: What does self-attention help a computer understand?

options.text: How words in a sentence relate to each other
How to spell long words correctly
How to count the number of letters in a paragraph

exercises.explain: Self-attention helps computers understand the relationship between words in a sentence by letting them interact.

cards.title: Words depend on their neighbors!
cards.body: Have you ever noticed how the meaning of a word changes depending on its neighbors? A "crane" could be a bird or a construction machine. Computers need a way to look at the whole sentence to figure out the right meaning. Let's see how self-attention does this!

cards.title: What is Self-Attention?
cards.body: Self-attention is a mechanism where every single word in a sentence looks at all the other words to update its own meaning. Instead of static dictionary definitions, words get custom, context-rich vectors based on their active neighbors.

cards.title: The Cocktail Party
cards.body: Think of self-attention like a group of people at a party. Instead of standing in isolated corners, everyone looks around the room, chats with others, and adjusts their behavior based on who else is there. By the end of the night, everyone's experience is shaped by the conversations they had.

cards.title: Context in Action
cards.body: In the sentence "The bank of the river was muddy," the word "bank" looks at "river" and updates its vector to mean land, not money. In "The bank approved my loan," the word "bank" looks at "loan" and updates its vector to mean a financial building. Same word, totally different context-driven vectors!

cards.title: Myth: Only Neighboring Words Count
cards.body: Many people think self-attention only looks at the words directly next to it (like left and right). In reality, it looks at every word in the entire sentence all at once, no matter how far apart they are!

cards.title: Static vs. Dynamic Words
cards.body: See how the way we represent words changes when we look at the whole sentence instead of just single words.

right.text: The word 'bank' updates its meaning dynamically based on 'river' or 'money'.

left.text: The word 'bank' has one fixed definition, regardless of the words around it.

exercises.prompt: What actually happens during a self-attention step?

options.text: Every word updates its vector by looking at all other words in the sentence.
It only looks at the very first word of the sentence.
It randomly deletes half of the words in the sentence.
It translates the words into a different language instantly.

exercises.explain: Self-attention allows every word in a sentence to interact with and learn from every other word at the same time.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Self-attention can only connect words that are right next to each other.
exercises.explain: Self-attention evaluates the relationships between all words in a sentence simultaneously, not just adjacent ones.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of self-attention.
exercises.sentence: Self-attention updates a word's vector by looking at its surrounding [blank].
exercises.explain: Self-attention uses the surrounding context (other words in the sentence) to update and refine the meaning of each word vector.

exercises.prompt: Which of these shows an AI successfully using self-attention on the word 'bat' in: 'The bat flew into the night'?

options.text: A system that defines 'bat' only as a wooden stick, ignoring the rest of the sentence.
An AI that connects 'bat' to 'wings' and 'night' because they appear in the same sentence.
An algorithm that randomly pairs words without looking at the sentence structure.

exercises.explain: The second sentence uses a natural, context-aware AI representation where 'bat' is connected to 'flying animal' because of the word 'night'. The others are rigid or unrelated.

exercises.prompt: How does self-attention build a rich representation of a sentence?

options.text: It ignores surrounding words and processes each word completely in isolation.
It updates each word's representation by blending information from its neighbors.
It deletes all other words in the sentence to save computer memory.
It forces the computer to translate the sentence into numbers using a dictionary.

exercises.explain: Superb job! You've mastered how self-attention dynamically updates word vectors by looking at the entire context at once. Next up: Seq2Seq Models.
